Hearing Re: Plaintiffs' Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to quash DENIED and motion for protective order GRANTED in part. Respondent may discover medical records related to those conditions (not body parts) that Claimant asserts are associated with the accident. Claimant's suggestion of 5 years is reasonable and should provide sufficient information that Respondent's experts can evaluate whether there are preexisting conditions or other factors that may be relevant to assessing damages. Thus, Respondent may discover medical records between October 2013 and present. Respondent may discover the employment records from October 2013 to present. Claimant's response to FROG 8.1 says that he is not making a claim for lost wages "at the present time," which suggests that he may amend to recover such damages. Moreover, employment records may be relevant to other damages issues. The requests for sanctions by both parties are DENIED because both parties acted with substantial justification.

Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.
